description: Vendor-level metadata, not per-draft scoring. Four binary deal-breakers measured once at vendor onboarding and refreshed quarterly.
sub_criteria: Privilege-preserving evaluation (does the tool honor attorney-work-product doctrine on inputs)
No-train-on-data (contractual + technical guarantee that inputs are not used for model training)
Air-gap / VPC option (tool deployable without external data egress)

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: All four deal-breakers met with documented controls (DPA, SOC 2 Type II, deployment options, log retention spec).

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Three of four deal-breakers met; fourth committed on roadmap with ETA.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Two of four deal-breakers met. Two are deal-breakers for regulated industries but tool is usable in some configurations.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: One of four deal-breakers met. Tool usable only in low-stakes contexts (small entity, non-strategic disclosures).

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Zero deal-breakers met. Tool is not deployable for confidential client work.

track_b_protocol: Captured via vendor onboarding form; verified against DPA, SOC 2 / ISO 27001 reports, and deployment documentation. Refreshed quarterly. Track B reviewers do not score this dimension on a per-draft basis.
